LAW AND MOTION 302, MTN TO ENTER JUDGMENT (CODE CIV. PROC. SEC 664.6), IS GRANTED. THE EVIDENCE INDICATES THAT DEFENDANT HAS BREACHED SECTION 6(B) OF THE PARTIES' SETTLEMENT BY FAILING TO PROVIDE AN ACCURATE CAPITALIZATION TABLE TO PLAINTIFF. THE DECLARATION OF ANGELICA RELUCIO SHOWS THAT SHE HAS AN OWNERSHIP INTEREST IN DEFENDANT AND THAT INFORMATION WAS NEVER DISCLOSED TO THE PLAINTIFF. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Seal Records Lodged Conditionally Under Seal
Off Calendar Feb-19-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. PHILIP BARILOVITS of Severson & Werson, PC, Tel No. (415) 677-5501 appearing for Plaintiff Phoenix Lake, Inc.;
Atty. KIMBERLY P. ZAPATA of Beck, Bismonte & Finley, LLP, Tel No. (408) 938-7900 appearing for Defendant Globe Protect, Inc.

RULING - Matter was not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF PHOENIX LAKE, INC.'S Motion To Seal Records Lodged Conditionally Under Seal is denied. Plaintiff has withdrawn most of that motion and the motion fails to demonstrate good cause to seal any documents in compliance with CRC 2.550 et seq. DOCUMENTS LODGED BY PLAINTIFF ON 12/1/14 ARE RETURNED TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IN OPEN COURT. DOCUMENTS LODGED BY DEFENDANT ON 1/14/15 ARE RETURNED TO DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Enter Judgment (Code Civ. Proc. Sec 664.6)
Off Calendar Feb-19-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. PHILIP BARILOVITS of Severson & Werson, PC, Tel No. (415) 677-5501 appearing for Plaintiff Phoenix Lake, Inc.;
Atty. KIMBERLY P. ZAPATA of Beck, Bismonte & Finley, LLP, Tel No. (408) 938-7900 appearing for Defendant Globe Protect, Inc.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF PHOENIX LAKE, INC.'S Motion To Enter Judgment (Code Civ. Proc. Sec 664.6) is granted. The evidence indicates that defendant has breached Section 6(b) of the parties' settlement by failing to provide an accurate capitalization table to plaintiff. The declaration of Angelica Relucio shows that she has an ownership interest in defendant and that information was never disclosed to the plaintiff. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Dismissal
Off Calendar Sep-26-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. PHILIP BARILOVITS OF SEVERSON & WERSON, TEL. NO. 677-5501 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ALFREDO A. BISMONTE OF BECK LLP, TEL, NO. (408)938-7900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF PHOENIX LAKE, INC'S Motion To Vacate Dismissal is Granted. Paragraph 13 of the Settlement Agreement establishes the parties' intent to have the Court retain jurisdiction for enforcement. (Declaration of David Ericksen, Ex. B.) Plaintiff has established that the filing of the voluntary dismissal was an excusable mistake. The dismissal is therefore set aside pursuant to CCP sec. 473(b). (See Zamora v. Clayborn Contracting Group, Inc. (2002) 28 Cal.4th 249, holding that the court may set aside a voluntary dismissal under the discretionary provision of section 473(b).) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Dismissal

Defendant may file a substantive opposition and Plaintiff may file a reply brief in accordance with CCP sec. 1005(b) contemplating the new hearing date. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/mjm
